fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 73 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(active) == limit` the function incorrectly returns `True` (allowed) instead of `False` (throttled).

constraints.intent: When active count equals the limit the branch condition should be true (deny), but the buggy `>` makes it false.
The else branch (allowed=True) should only be reached when active count is strictly less than the limit.
Sanity check that the throttle decision covers the equality case: active == limit must map to denied.
If the action is allowed (True), the active count must be strictly less than the limit; equality should produce denied.
